NCT03345849.name: U-EXCEL
NCT03345849.group: Moderate-severe CD (biologic-naive or -exposed), upadacitinib 45 mg PO QD induction vs placebo (wk 12, clinical remission)
NCT03345849.snippet: Source: Loftus EV Jr et al. N Engl J Med 2023;388:1966-1980 (U-EXCEL induction).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 526 adults with moderate-to-severe Crohn disease (CDAI 220-450) with inadequate response to conventional or biologic therapy randomized 2:1 double-blind to upadacitinib 45 mg PO daily (n=263) or matching placebo (n=263) for 12-week induction. Baseline biologic-naive and -exposed patients included.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Clinical Remission at Week 12
evidence.text: Clinical remission (CDAI <150) at week 12: upadacitinib 130/263 (49.5%) vs placebo 77/263 (29.1%); odds ratio 2.29 (95% CI 1.57 to 3.34; P<0.0001). Co-primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Endoscopic Response at Week 12
evidence.text: Endoscopic response (SES-CD reduction >=50%) at week 12: upadacitinib 120/263 (45.5%) vs placebo 34/263 (13.1%); odds ratio 5.42 (95% CI 3.50 to 8.42; P<0.0001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IRT randomization stratified by biologic-exposure and baseline CDAI. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 91% induction completion. D4 LOW - central blinded endoscopy (SES-CD). D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Confirmed diagnosis of CD for at least 3 months prior to Baseline.~* Confirmed diagnosis of moderate to severe CD as assessed by stool frequency (SF), abdominal pain (AP) score.~* Evidence of mucosal inflammation based on the Simplified Endoscopic Score for Crohn's disease (SES-CD) on an endoscopy confirmed by a central reader.~* Demonstrated an inadequate response or intolerance to one or more conventional and/or biologic therapies (oral locally acting steroids, intraveno...

NCT03345836.name: U-EXCEED
NCT03345836.group: Moderate-severe CD biologic-failure, upadacitinib 45 mg PO QD induction vs placebo (wk 12)
NCT03345836.snippet: Source: Loftus EV Jr et al. N Engl J Med 2023;388:1966-1980 (U-EXCEED induction biologic-failure).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 495 adults with moderate-to-severe Crohn disease who had failed or were intolerant of >=1 biologic therapy randomized 2:1 double-blind to upadacitinib 45 mg PO daily (n=247) or matching placebo (n=248) for 12-week induction.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Clinical Remission at Week 12
evidence.text: Clinical remission at week 12 (biologic-failure): upadacitinib 96/247 (38.9%) vs placebo 52/248 (21.1%); odds ratio 2.44 (95% CI 1.65 to 3.62; P<0.0001). Co-primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Endoscopic Response at Week 12
evidence.text: Endoscopic response at week 12 in biologic-failure: upadacitinib 86/247 (34.6%) vs placebo 9/248 (3.5%); odds ratio 14.29 (95% CI 6.91 to 29.56; P<0.0001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization stratified by prior biologic number and CDAI. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 90% completion. D4 LOW - central blinded endoscopy. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Confirmed diagnosis of CD for at least 3 months prior to Baseline.~* Confirmed diagnosis of moderate to severe CD as assessed by stool frequency (SF), abdominal pain (AP) score.~* Evidence of mucosal inflammation based on the Simplified Endoscopic Score for Crohn's disease (SES-CD) on an endoscopy confirmed by a central reader.~* Demonstrated an inadequate response or intolerance to any biologic therapy for infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab pegol, vedolizumab, and ustek...

NCT03345823.name: U-ENDURE
NCT03345823.group: CD wk12 responders, upadacitinib 30 mg PO QD vs placebo maintenance (wk 52)
NCT03345823.snippet: Source: Loftus EV Jr et al. N Engl J Med 2023;388:1966-1980 (U-ENDURE maintenance).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 502 Crohn-disease patients who had achieved clinical response to upadacitinib IV/oral induction re-randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to maintenance with upadacitinib 30 mg PO daily (n=252), upadacitinib 15 mg PO daily (n=251), or placebo (n=250) for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Clinical Remission at Week 52
evidence.text: Clinical remission (CDAI <150) at week 52: upadacitinib 30 mg 94/252 (37.3%) vs placebo 34/250 (13.7%); odds ratio 3.77 (95% CI 2.40 to 5.91; P<0.0001). Co-primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Endoscopic Response at Week 52
evidence.text: Endoscopic response at week 52: upadacitinib 30 mg 70/252 (27.6%) vs placebo 18/250 (7.3%); odds ratio 4.62 (95% CI 2.62 to 8.14; P<0.0001). Durable endoscopic benefit.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central re-randomization stratified by induction dose and biologic failure. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 85% completion. D4 LOW - blinded central endoscopy. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~For Substudy 1:~* Participant who achieve clinical response in Study M14-431 or Study M14-433.~* Participant completes study procedures in the parent study. The final endoscopy for Studies M14-431 or M14-433 may be missing, if the endoscopy cannot be performed during the COVID-19 pandemic.~For Substudy 2:~* Participant completes Substudy 1. title: RapidMeta Gastroenterology | Upadacitinib for Moderate-to-Severe Crohn Disease v1.1
